# Amazon S3 Connector - Full Documentation

# Amazon S3 Connector 8.0 Additional Configuration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Before You Begin for Source Operations

Amazon S3 source operations use Amazon SQS for notifying when a new object is created or deleted in
an S3 bucket.

There are multiple scenarios for the **On New Object** and **On Deleted Object** Amazon S3 sources:

1.  The [queue is already created](#queue-is-created) and specified
2.  The [queue is created](#queue-created-by-source) by the source
3.  The [event notification configuration is created](#configuration-is-created) by the source

## Queue Is Already Created

To correctly use a source for a queue that already exists, the user must have these actions enabled
for the Amazon SQS queue:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "VisualEditor0",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "sqs:DeleteMessage",
        "sqs:GetQueueUrl",
        "sqs:ReceiveMessage",
        "sqs:GetQueueAttributes"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:sqs:REGION:ACCOUNT_ID:QUEUE_NAME"
    }
  ]
}
```

Additionally, to enable the queue to receive messages when a new object is created or deleted in an
S3 bucket, create a policy for the queue in this format:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Id": "arn:aws:sqs:REGION:ACCOUNT_ID:QUEUE_NAME/SQSDefaultPolicy",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "Sid1593770403887",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Action": "SQS:SendMessage",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:sqs:REGION:ACCOUNT_ID:QUEUE_NAME",
      "Condition": {
        "ArnLike": {
          "aws:SourceArn": "arn:aws:s3:::BUCKET_NAME"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Queue Is Created by Source

If the queue is created by the source, the queue name has the prefix `MULE-S3-TRIGGER`.

When the queue is created by the source, the user must have permission to both create a queue and
set the queue attributes:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "VisualEditor0",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "sqs:DeleteMessage",
        "sqs:ReceiveMessage",
        "sqs:GetQueueAttributes",
        "sqs:CreateQueue",
        "sqs:SetQueueAttributes",
        "sqs:GetQueueUrl"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:sqs:REGION:ACCOUNT_ID:MULE-S3-TRIGGER-*"
    }
  ]
}
```

When the queue is created by the source, an SQS policy is added to the queue in the following format
when the S3 object is created or deleted in the bucket :

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Id": "example-ID",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "example-statement-ID",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": {
        "AWS": "*"
    },
      "Action": "SQS:SendMessage",
      "Resource":QUEUE_ARN,
      "Condition": {
        "ArnLike": {
          "aws:SourceArn": "BUCKET_ARN"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Notification Configuration for S3 Bucket Events is Already Created

To correctly use a source for a notification configuration that already exists, you must specify the
notification configuration name in the ADVANCED tab of the source:

```xml
   <s3:new-object-trigger doc:name="On New Object" config-ref="${config}" bucketName="example-bucket-name"
                               notificationConfigurationName="on-new-object-configuration">
            <scheduling-strategy>
                <fixed-frequency/>
            </scheduling-strategy>
        </s3:new-object-trigger>
```

## S3 Policy Bucket

For the S3 policy, use this format for all cases:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Id": "Policy1593761427184",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "Stmt1593760119344",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": {
        "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT_ID:user/test"
      },
      "Action": "SPECIFIC_ACTION",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::BUCKET_NAME/*"
    },
    {
      "Sid": "Stmt1593760259223",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": {
        "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT_ID:user/test"
      },
      "Action": ["s3:PutBucketNotification", "s3:GetBucketNotification"],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::BUCKET_NAME"
    }
  ]
}
```

In this example, the **SPECIFIC_ACTION** is `s3:PutObject` or `s3:DeleteObject`, depending on the
specified source operation.

## Next Step

After you complete configuring the connector, you can try the
[Example](amazon-s3-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon S3 Connector 8.0 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following example shows how to store an image from a URL in Amazon S3, and retrieve and display
the image.

## Before You Begin

For this example to work in Anypoint Studio, you must provide Amazon Web Services credentials. You
can either:

- Replace the variables with their values in the code.
- Provide the values for each variable in the `src/main/resources/mule-artifact.properties` file.

## Example Steps

![Studio 7 Visual Studio Icon Flow. The flow shows HTTP Listener, Create bucket, Get MuleSoft logo, Create logo object in S3 bucket, Get Image, Delete object and Delete S3 bucket.](_images/flow.png)

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  Drag an **HTTP > Listener** into the canvas, and select it to open the properties editor.
3.  Add a new HTTP Listener Config global element:
    1.  In **General Settings**, click the **+** button:
    2.  Configure the following HTTP parameters, retain the default values for the other fields, and
        click **OK**:

        <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP_Listener_Configuration</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>127.0.0.1</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>8081</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    3.  Reference the **HTTP Listener Configuration** global element. In the General tab, specify
        the `/` Path.

4.  Drag an Amazon S3 connector into the flow, and double-click the connector to open its properties
    editor.
5.  If you donât have an existing Amazon S3 connector global element to choose, click the **+** sign
    next to **Extension Configuration** field.
6.  Configure the global element properties, and then click **OK**.
7.  Configure the connector parameters:

    ![Connector Properties General tab](_images/s3_config.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector instance, such as <code>Amazon_S3_Configuration</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>General</p></td><td><p>General configuration for the connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><p>Region, for example, <code>us-east-1</code> (default).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access key</p></td><td><p>Connection access key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret key</p></td><td><p>Connection secret key.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

8.  Drag the **Create Bucket** operation into the flow.

    ![S3 Connector Create Bucket in S3 General Tab](_images/create_bucket.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Display name of your choice, such as <code>Create new bucket</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration for the connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Bucket name</p></td><td><p>Name of the newly created bucket.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ACL</p></td><td><p>PRIVATE (default) access control list. A canned ACL is a predefined grant.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

9.  Add an HTTP > Connector to request the MuleSoft logo from MuleSoft.

    ![HTTP Connector General Tab](_images/request.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector instance. In this example, HTTP &gt; Connector is named <code>Get MuleSoft logo</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>Configuration with the <strong>Host</strong> as <code>developer.mulesoft.com</code>, <strong>Protocol</strong> as <code>HTTPS</code>, and <strong>Port</strong> as <code>80</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL or Path</p></td><td><p><code>/sites/all/themes/muletheme/images/mulesoft_dev_logo_v2.svg</code>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

10. Drag the **Put Object** operation into the flow to create the requested MuleSoft logo in the
    selected Amazon S3 bucket.

    ![S3 Connector Create Logo Object in S3 Bucket General Tab](_images/put_object.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Display name of your choice, such as <code>Create logo object in S3 bucket</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector Configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration for the connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Bucket Name</p></td><td><p>Name of the bucket in which the new object is created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><p>Name of the newly created object.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><p>Content of the object.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object ACL</p></td><td><p>Access control list.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

11. Drag the **Get Object** operation into the flow to get the newly created MuleSoft logo image
    object from the bucket.

    ![S3 Connector Get Image General Tab](_images/get_object.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Display name of your choice, such as <code>Get Image</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector Configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration for the connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Bucket Name</p></td><td><p>Name of the bucket in which an object is stored.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Key</p></td><td><p>Name of the object to get.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

12. Drag the **Delete Object** operation into the flow to delete the object. Because the **Delete
    Object** operationâs return type is void, the payload contains the object returned by the **Get
    Object** operation.

    ![S3 Connector Delete S3 Bucket General Tab](_images/delete_object.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Display name of your choice, such as <code>Delete created object</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector Configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration for the connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Bucket Name</p></td><td><p>Name of the bucket in which an object is stored.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Name</p></td><td><p>Name of the object to delete.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

13. Drag the **Delete Bucket** operation into the flow to delete the bucket. Because the **Delete
    Bucket** operationâs return type is void, the payload contains the object returned by the **Get
    Object** operation.

    ![S3 Connector Delete S3 Bucket General Tab](_images/delete_bucket.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Display name of your choice, such as <code>Delete created bucket</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector Configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration for the connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Bucket Name</p></td><td><p>Name of the bucket to delete.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## XML for the Example

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:s3="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s3"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s3 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s3/current/mule-s3.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
		doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="DOC_ID">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
			port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<configuration-properties doc:name="Configuration properties" doc:id="DOC_ID" file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<s3:config name="Amazon_S3_Configuration" doc:name="Amazon S3 Configuration" doc:id="DOC_ID" >
		<s3:connection accessKey="${config.accessKey}" secretKey="${config.secretKey}" />
	</s3:config>
	<http:request-config name="HTTP_Request_configuration" doc:name="HTTP Request configuration" doc:id="DOC_ID">
		<http:request-connection protocol="HTTPS" host="developer.mulesoft.com"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<flow name="docu-demoFlow" doc:id="DOC_ID" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="DOC_ID" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/"/>
		<s3:create-bucket doc:name="Create new bucket" doc:id="DOC_ID" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" bucketName="${bucket.name}" acl="PRIVATE"/>
		<http:request method="GET" doc:name="Get Mulesoft logo" doc:id="DOC_ID" path="/sites/all/themes/muletheme/images/mulesoft_dev_logo_v2.svg" config-ref="HTTP_Request_configuration"/>
		<s3:put-object doc:name="Create logo object in S3 bucket" doc:id="DOC_ID" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" bucketName="${bucket.name}" key="${file.name}" objectACL="PRIVATE"/>
		<s3:get-object doc:name="Get image" doc:id="DOC_ID" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" bucketName="${bucket.name}" key="${file.name}"/>
		<s3:delete-object doc:name="Delete created object" doc:id="DOC_ID" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" bucketName="${bucket.name}" key="${file.name}"/>
		<s3:delete-bucket doc:name="Delete created bucket" doc:id="DOC_ID" config-ref="Amazon_S3_Configuration" bucketName="${bucket.name}"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Amazon Default Provider Credential Chain](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/v1/developer-guide/credentials.html)
- [Amazon Access Control List (ACL) overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/acl-overview.html)
---

# Amazon S3 Connector 8.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Amazon S3 (Amazon S3 Connector) provides connectivity to the Amazon S3 API,
enabling you to interface with Amazon S3 to store objects, download and use data with other AWS
services, and build applications that require internet storage.

## Configuration

Default Configuration for Amazon S3 Connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_Connection">Connection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection (S3) Connection Type

Uses the S3 connection type, which covers both the Basic and Role connections.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic region endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>us-east-1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. A value of <code>0</code> means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>50</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Role</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Role">Role</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Role configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, obtains credentials from the AWS environment. For more information, refer to <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">Provide Temporary Credentials to the SDK. If you enable this option, the SDK manages the token renewal because the connector doesnât manage the credentials. </a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum number of outbound connections that are kept open at the same time. Unlimited by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>-1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Service Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom service endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint. For local connections, set this field to <code>http://localhost:8000/</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom SQS Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom SQS endpoint. Useful when a non-standard SQS endpoint (used by sources) is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Proxy connection settings for outbound connections. This setting applies to the sources and checkpoint operation. It does not affect the put record operation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

> [!IMPORTANT] You must perform additional configuration to use the sources provided by Amazon S3
> Connector. Refer to the [prerequisites](amazon-s3-connector-config-topics#prereq) before
> configuring your sources.

- [On Deleted Object](#DeletedObjectListener)
- [On New Object](#NewObjectListener)

### On Deleted Object

`<s3:deleted-object-listener>`

> [!IMPORTANT] You must perform additional configuration to use the sources provided by Amazon S3
> Connector. Refer to the [prerequisites](amazon-s3-connector-config-topics#prereq) before
> configuring your sources.

Initiates a Mule flow when an object is deleted from an Amazon S3 bucket, and sends an SQS
notification about the deleted object.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket to which the source listens for changes.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the folder in the specified bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amazon SQS queue name used to notify users when a new object is created or deleted in an S3 bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the notification configuration to use in the source. The notification configuration must exist and must be of Event Type 's3:ObjectCreated:*' with the <strong>On New Object</strong> source and of Event Type 's3:ObjectRemoved:*' with the <strong>On Deleted Object</strong> source. Configurations with events of the same type cannot have overlapping prefix or suffix configurations.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#S3ObjectTriggerResponse">S3 Object Trigger Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

### On New Object

`<s3:new-object-listener>`

> [!IMPORTANT] You must perform additional configuration to use the sources provided by Amazon S3
> Connector. Refer to the [prerequisites](amazon-s3-connector-config-topics#prereq) before
> configuring your sources.

Initiates a Mule flow when an object is created in an Amazon S3 bucket, and sends an SQS
notification about the new object.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default of the folder in the specified bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the name of the notification configuration to use in the source. The notification configuration must exist and must be of Event Type 's3:ObjectCreated:*' with the <strong>On New Object</strong> source and of Event Type 's3:ObjectRemoved:*' with the <strong>On Deleted Object</strong> source. ### Abort Multipart Upload

`<s3:abort-multipart-upload>`

Aborts a multipart upload. After a multipart upload is aborted, no additional parts can be uploaded
using that upload ID. The storage consumed by any previously uploaded parts will be freed. However,
if any part uploads are currently in progress, those part uploads might or might not succeed. As a
result, it might be necessary to abort a given multipart upload multiple times to completely free
all storage consumed by all parts.

To verify that all parts are removed to avoid getting charged for the part storage, call the
[ListParts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/API_ListParts.html) action and ensure
that the parts list is empty. For information about permissions required to use the multipart
upload, refer to
[Multipart Upload and Permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/mpuoverview.html).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name containing the multipart upload.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upload Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Upload ID that identifies the multipart upload.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_UPLOAD
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Complete Multipart Upload

`<s3:complete-multipart-upload>`

Completes a multipart upload by assembling previously uploaded parts.

You first initiate the multipart upload and then upload all parts using the **Upload Part**
operation. After successfully uploading all relevant parts of an upload, call this operation to
complete the upload.

Upon receiving this request, Amazon S3 concatenates all the parts in ascending order by part number
to create a new object. In the **Complete Multipart Upload** request, you must provide the parts
list and ensure that itâs complete.

This operation concatenates the parts that you provide in the list. For each part in the list, you
must provide the part number and ETag value, which is returned after that part is uploaded.

Processing of a **Complete Multipart Upload** request could take several minutes to complete. After
Amazon S3 begins processing the request, it sends an HTTP response header that specifies a `200 OK`
response. While processing is in progress, Amazon S3 periodically sends white space characters to
keep the connection from timing out. Because a request could fail after the initial `200 OK`
response has been sent, it is important that you check the response body to determine whether the
request succeeded.

Note that if **Complete Multipart Upload** fails, applications must be prepared to retry the failed
requests.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name in which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upload Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Upload ID that identifies the initiated multipart upload.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Completed Parts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#MultipartPart">Multipart Part</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of completed parts.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) matches the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) is different from the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#CompleteMultipartUploadResponse">Complete Multipart Upload Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_UPLOAD
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Copy Object

`<s3:copy-object>`

Creates a copy of an object that is already stored in Amazon S3.

You can store individual objects of up to 5 TB in Amazon S3. You create a copy of your object up to
5 GB in size in a single atomic action using this API. However, to copy an object greater than 5 GB,
you must use the multipart upload **Upload Part - Copy API**. For more information, refer to
[Copy Object Using the REST Multipart Upload API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/CopyingObjectsMPUapi.html).

All copy requests must be authenticated. Additionally, you must have read access to the source
object and write access to the destination bucket. For more information, refer to
[REST Authentication](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTAuthentication.html).

Both the region that you want to copy the object from and the region that you want to copy the
object to must be enabled for your account. A copy request might return an error when Amazon S3
receives the copy request or while Amazon S3 is copying the files. If the error occurs before the
copy action starts, you receive a standard Amazon S3 error. If the error occurs during the copy
operation, the error response is embedded in the `200 OK` response. This means that a `200 OK`
response can indicate either success or an error.

Design your application to parse the contents of the response and handle it appropriately. If the
copy is successful, you receive a response with information about the copied object. If the request
is an HTTP 1.1 request, the response is chunk encoded. Otherwise, it does not contain the
Content-Length, and you must read the entire body. The copy request charge is based on the storage
class and region that you specify for the destination object. For pricing information, refer to
[Amazon S3 pricing](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/pricing/).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the source bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the source key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source version ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID of the source object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the destination bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key of the destination object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination ACL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PRIVATE</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ_WRITE</p></li><li><p>AWS_EXEC_READ</p></li><li><p>AUTHENTICATED_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_FULL_CONTROL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Access control list (ACL) settings of the copied object. The ACL is not preserved and is set to private for the user making the request. To override the default ACL setting, specify a new ACL when generating a copy request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination storage class</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>STANDARD</p></li><li><p>REDUCED_REDUNDANCY</p></li><li><p>STANDARD_IA</p></li><li><p>ONEZONE_IA</p></li><li><p>INTELLIGENT_TIERING</p></li><li><p>GLACIER</p></li><li><p>DEEP_ARCHIVE</p></li><li><p>OUTPOSTS</p></li><li><p>GLACIER_IR</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Storage class of the new object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination user metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When copying an object, you can preserve all metadata (default) or specify new metadata.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Modified since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Copies the object only if the object is modified after the specified date.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Unmodified since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Copies the object only if the object is not modified after the specified date.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Server Side Encryption</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server-side encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Represents a customer-provided key to use with Amazon S3 server-side encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Kms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AWS Key Management System key ID used for Server Side Encryption of the Amazon S3 object. You must specify the <strong>Server Side Encryption</strong> field when you set the <strong>Sse Kms Key Id</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Key MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the base64-encoded 128-bit MD5 digest of the encryption key according to RFC 1321. Amazon S3 uses this header for a message integrity check to ensure that the encryption key is transmitted without error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the algorithm to use to when encrypting the object. Valid values are AES256.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aws:kms</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>ContentType</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard MIME type describing the format of the object data.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#CopyObjectResponse">Copy Object Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:INVALID_OBJECT_STATE
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Create Bucket

`<s3:create-bucket>`

Creates a new S3 bucket. To create a bucket, you must register with Amazon S3 and have a valid AWS
access key ID to authenticate requests. Anonymous requests are never allowed to create buckets. By
creating the bucket, you become the bucket owner.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Location Constraint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the region in which the bucket is created.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acl</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PRIVATE</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ_WRITE</p></li><li><p>AUTHENTICATED_READ</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Access control list (ACL) settings of the created bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:BUCKET_ALREADY_EXISTS
- S3:BUCKET_ALREADY_OWNED_BY_YOU
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Create Multipart Upload

`<s3:create-multipart-upload>`

Initiates a multipart upload and returns an upload ID. This upload ID is used to associate all of
the parts in the specific multipart upload. You specify this upload ID in each of your subsequent
upload part requests (refer to [Upload Part](#UploadPart)).

You also include this upload ID in the final request to either complete or abort the multipart
upload request. For more information about multipart uploads, refer to
[Multipart Upload Overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/mpuoverview.html).

If you configure a lifecycle rule to abort incomplete multipart uploads, the upload must complete
within the number of days specified in the bucket lifecycle configuration. Otherwise, the incomplete
multipart upload becomes eligible for an abort action and Amazon S3 aborts the multipart upload. For
more information, refer to
[Aborting Incomplete Multipart Uploads Using a Bucket Lifecycle Policy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/mpuoverview.html).

For information about the permissions required to use the multipart upload API, refer to
[Uploading and Copying Objects Using Multipart Upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/mpuoverview.html).
After you initiate a multipart upload and upload one or more parts, to stop being charged for
storing the uploaded parts, you must either complete or abort the multipart upload. Amazon S3 frees
up the space used to store the parts and stops charging you for storing them only after you either
complete or abort a multipart upload.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket in which to initiate the upload.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object metadata.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object ACL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PRIVATE</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ_WRITE</p></li><li><p>AWS_EXEC_READ</p></li><li><p>AUTHENTICATED_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_FULL_CONTROL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Canned ACL to apply to the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#CreateMultipartUploadResponse">Create Multipart Upload Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Create Object Presigned Url

`<s3:create-object-presigned-url>`

Returns a presigned URL to access an Amazon S3 object. The presigned URL can be shared to other
users, which enables access to the resource without providing an accountâs AWS security credentials.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket in the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key of the object in the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Part Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required if <strong>Method</strong> is <code>UPLOAD_PART</code>. If the content size is equal to or less than 5 MB, then <strong>Part Number</strong> is <code>1</code>. If not, use a loop to increase the number (1, 2, 3, and so on).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upload Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Required if <strong>Method</strong> is <code>ABORT_MULTIPART_UPLOAD</code>, <code>COMPLETE_MULTIPART_UPLOAD</code>, or <code>UPLOAD_PART</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Method</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ABORT_MULTIPART_UPLOAD</p></li><li><p>COMPLETE_MULTIPART_UPLOAD</p></li><li><p>CREATE_MULTIPART_UPLOAD</p></li><li><p>GET_OBJECT</p></li><li><p>PUT_OBJECT</p></li><li><p>UPLOAD_PART</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of object operation to presign.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>PUT_OBJECT</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Duration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the presigned URL is valid.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>3600</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Duration Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit of the <strong>Duration</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base64 encoded 128-bit MD5 digest of the associated object (content - not including headers) according to RFC 1864.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard MIME type describing the format of the contents.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server-side encryption (SSE) algorithm used for the presigned URL. This field is not allowed for the HTTP GET method. If you set a value for this field and use the HTTP GET method, you will receive an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Delete Bucket

`<s3:delete-bucket>`

Deletes the S3 bucket. All objects (including all object versions and delete markers) in the bucket
must be deleted before the bucket itself can be deleted.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Delete Bucket Cors

`<s3:delete-bucket-cors>`

Deletes the Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) configuration information set for the bucket. To
use this operation, you must have permission to perform the s3:PutBucketCORS action. The bucket
owner has this permission by default and can grant this permission to others.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Delete Bucket Lifecycle Configuration

`<s3:delete-bucket-lifecycle-configuration>`

Deletes the lifecycle configuration from the specified bucket. Amazon S3 removes all the lifecycle
configuration rules in the lifecycle subresource associated with the bucket. Your objects never
expire, and Amazon S3 no longer automatically deletes any objects on the basis of rules contained in
the deleted lifecycle configuration.

To use this operation, you must have permission to perform the s3:PutLifecycleConfiguration action.
By default, the bucket owner has this permission and the bucket owner can grant this permission to
others.

There is usually some time lag before lifecycle configuration deletion is fully propagated to all
the Amazon S3 systems.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_LIFECYCLE_CONFIGURATION
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Delete Bucket Policy

`<s3:delete-bucket-policy>`

This implementation of the DELETE action uses the policy subresource to delete the policy of a
specified bucket. If you are using an identity other than the root user of the AWS account that owns
the bucket, the calling identity must have the DeleteBucketPolicy permissions on the specified
bucket and must belong to the bucket ownerâs account to use this operation.

If you donât have the DeleteBucketPolicy permissions, Amazon S3 returns a `403` Access Denied error.
If you have the correct permissions, but youâre not using an identity that belongs to the bucket
ownerâs account, Amazon S3 returns a `405 Method Not Allowed` error.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Delete Bucket Tagging

`<s3:delete-bucket-tagging>`

Deletes the tags from the bucket. To use this operation, you must have permission to perform the
s3:PutBucketTagging action. By default, the bucket owner has this permission and can grant this
permission to others.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Delete Bucket Website

`<s3:delete-bucket-website>`

Removes the website configuration from a bucket.

Amazon S3 returns a `200 OK` response upon successfully deleting a website configuration from the
specified bucket. You receive a `200 OK` response if the website configuration you are trying to
delete does not exist on the bucket. Amazon S3 returns a 404 response if the bucket specified in the
request does not exist.

This DELETE action requires the S3:DeleteBucketWebsite permission. By default, only the bucket owner
can delete the website configuration attached to a bucket. However, bucket owners can grant other
users permission to delete the website configuration by writing a bucket policy granting them the
S3:DeleteBucketWebsite permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Delete Object

`<s3:delete-object>`

Removes the null version (if there is one) of an object and inserts a delete marker, which becomes
the latest version of the object. If there isnât a null version, Amazon S3 does not remove any
objects but still responds that the command was successful.

To remove a specific version, you must be the bucket owner and you must use the version ID
subresource. Using this subresource permanently deletes the version.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name containing the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key name to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID used to reference a specific version of the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Delete Objects

`<s3:delete-objects>`

Enables you to delete multiple objects from a bucket using a single HTTP request. If you know the
object keys that you want to delete, then this operation provides a suitable alternative to sending
individual delete requests, reducing per-request overhead.

The request contains a list of up to 1000 keys that you want to delete. In the XML, you provide the
object key names, and optionally, version IDs if you want to delete a specific version of the object
from a versioning-enabled bucket. For each key, Amazon S3 performs a delete action and returns the
result of that delete, success, or failure, in the response. If the object specified in the request
is not found, Amazon S3 returns the result as deleted.

The action supports two modes for the response: verbose and quiet. By default, the action uses the
verbose mode, in which the response includes the result of deletion of each key in your request. In
quiet mode, the response includes only keys where the delete action encounters an error. For a
successful deletion, the action does not return any information about the delete in the response
body.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name containing the objects to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object identifiers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ObjectIdentifier">Object Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of the object identifiers containing object keys and versions of the object to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Bucket Acl

`<s3:get-bucket-acl>`

This implementation of the GET action uses the ACL subresource to return the ACL of a bucket. To use
GET to return the ACL of the bucket, you must have READ_ACP access to the bucket. If the READ_ACP
permission is granted to the anonymous user, you can return the ACL of the bucket without using an
authorization header.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BucketAclResponse">Bucket Acl Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Bucket Cors

`<s3:get-bucket-cors>`

Returns the CORS configuration information set for the bucket. To use this operation, you must have
permission to perform the s3:GetBucketCORS action. By default, the bucket owner has this permission
and can grant it to others.

### Get Bucket Lifecycle Configuration

`<s3:get-bucket-lifecycle-configuration>`

Returns the lifecycle configuration information set on the bucket. To use this operation, you must
have permission to perform the s3:GetLifecycleConfiguration action. The bucket owner has this
permission, by default. The bucket owner can grant this permission to others.

`<s3:get-bucket-location>`

Returns the region the bucket resides in. You set the bucketâs region using the **Location
Constraint** request parameter in the **Create Bucket** operation. To use this implementation of the
operation, you must be the bucket owner.

### Get Bucket Logging

`<s3:get-bucket-logging>`

Returns the logging status of a bucket and the permissions users have to view and modify that
status. To use GET, you must be the bucket owner.

### Get Bucket Notification Configuration

`<s3:get-bucket-notification-configuration>`

Returns the notification configuration of a bucket. If notifications are not enabled on the bucket,
the action returns an empty NotificationConfiguration element. By default, you must be the bucket
owner to read the notification configuration of a bucket. However, the bucket owner can use a bucket
policy to grant permission to other users to read this configuration with the
s3:GetBucketNotification permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. - S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
