- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Bucket Policy

`<s3:get-bucket-policy>`

Returns the policy of a specified bucket. If you are using an identity other than the root user of
the AWS account that owns the bucket, the calling identity must have the GetBucketPolicy permissions
on the specified bucket and belong to the bucket ownerâs account to use this operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BucketPolicyResponse">Bucket Policy Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Bucket Tagging

`<s3:get-bucket-tagging>`

Returns the tag set associated with the bucket. To use this operation, you must have permission to
perform the s3:GetBucketTagging action. By default, the bucket owner has this permission and can
grant this permission to others.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BucketTaggingResponse">Bucket Tagging Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_TAG_SET
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Bucket Versioning

`<s3:get-bucket-versioning>`

Returns the versioning state of a bucket. To retrieve the versioning state of a bucket, you must be
the bucket owner. This implementation also returns the MFA Delete status of the versioning state. If
the MFA Delete status is enabled, the bucket owner must use an authentication device to change the
versioning state of the bucket.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Bucket Website

`<s3:get-bucket-website>`

Returns the website configuration for a bucket. To host a website on Amazon S3, you can configure a
bucket as a website by adding a website configuration. This GET action requires the
S3:GetBucketWebsite permission. By default, only the bucket owner can read the bucket website
configuration. However, bucket owners can allow other users to read the website configuration by
writing a bucket policy granting them the S3:GetBucketWebsite permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configuration](#config)

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Object

`<s3:get-object>`

Retrieves objects from Amazon S3. To use GET, you must have READ access to the object. If you grant
READ access to the anonymous user, you can return the object without using an authorization header.
If the object you are retrieving is stored in the S3 Glacier or S3 Glacier Deep Archive storage
class, or S3 Intelligent-Tiering Archive or S3 Intelligent-Tiering Deep Archive tiers, before you
can retrieve the object you must first restore a copy using the **Restore Object** operation.
Otherwise, this action returns an InvalidObjectState error. For more information about restoring
archived objects, refer to
[Restoring Archived Objects](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/restoring-objects.html).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name containing the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key to get.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Part Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Part number of the object to read. This is a positive integer between <code>1</code> and <code>10,000</code>. Specifying this number effectively performs a ranged GET request for the part specified, which is useful for downloading only a part of an object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Range</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Range of the bytes copied from the source object, from the first byte to the last byte.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#GetObjectOperationAttributes">Get Object Operation Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:INVALID_OBJECT_STATE
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Get Object ACL

`<s3:get-object-acl>`

Returns the ACL of an object. To use this operation, you must have s3:GetObjectAcl permissions or
READ_ACP access to the object. This action is not supported by Amazon S3 on Outposts.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name that contains the object for which to get the ACL information.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which to get the ACL information.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID used to reference a specific version of the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET

- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Get Object Attributes

`<s3:get-object-attributes>`

Retrieves all the metadata from an object without returning the object itself. This operation is
useful if youâre interested only in an objectâs metadata. To use this operation, you must have READ
access to the object.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name containing the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID used to reference a specific version of the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>E_TAG</p></li><li><p>CHECKSUM</p></li><li><p>OBJECT_PARTS</p></li><li><p>STORAGE_CLASS</p></li><li><p>OBJECT_SIZE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the fields at the root level that you want returned to the response. Fields that you do not specify are not returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Parts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the maximum number of parts to return.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Part Number Marker</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the part after which listing begins. Only parts with higher part numbers are listed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#GetObjectAttributesResponse">Get Object Attributes Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Head Object

`<s3:head-object>`

The HEAD action retrieves metadata from an object without returning the object itself. This action
is useful if you are interested only in an objectâs metadata. To use HEAD, you must have READ access
to the object. A HEAD request has the same options as a GET action on an object. The response is
identical to the GET response except that there is no response body.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket containing the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID used to reference a specific version of the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Part Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Part number of the object that is read. This is a positive integer between <code>1</code> and <code>10,000</code>. Effectively performs a ranged HEAD request for the part specified. Useful for querying the size of the part and the number of parts in the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) matches the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) is different from the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Modified since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if it was modified since the specified time.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Unmodified since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if it was not modified since the specified time.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#HeadObjectResponse">Head Object Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### List Buckets

`<s3:list-buckets>`

Returns a list of all buckets owned by the authenticated sender of the request. To use this
operation, you must have the s3:ListAllMyBuckets permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#ListBucketsResponse">List Buckets Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### List Multipart Uploads

`<s3:list-multipart-uploads>`

This operation lists in-progress multipart uploads. An in-progress multipart upload is a multipart
upload that is initiated using the **Initiate Multipart Upload** request, but that has not yet been
completed or aborted. In the response, the uploads are sorted by key. If your application has
initiated more than one multipart upload using the same object key, then uploads in the response are
first sorted by key. Additionally, uploads are sorted in ascending order within each key by the
upload initiation time. For information on permissions required to use the multipart upload API,
refer to
[Multipart Upload and Permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/mpuoverview.html).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket in which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefix</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limits the response to keys that begin with the specified prefix.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the page size of the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key marker</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the key to start with when listing parts in a bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upload Id marker</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the upload ID to start with when listing parts in a bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#MultipartUpload">Multipart Upload</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

### List Object Versions

`<s3:list-object-versions>`

Returns metadata about all versions of the objects in a bucket. You can also use request parameters
as selection criteria to return metadata about a subset of all the object versions. To use this
operation, you must have READ access to the bucket.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name to list.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Character you use to group keys.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Marker</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the key to start with when listing objects in a bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefix</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limits the response to keys that begin with the specified prefix.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the page size of the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Version Id Marker</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the object version you want to start listing from.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>URL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding type used by Amazon S3 to encode object keys in the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#ApiObjectVersion">Api Object Version</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

### List Objects

`<s3:list-objects>`

Returns all objects in a bucket. You can use the request parameters as selection criteria to return
a subset of the objects in a bucket. A `200 OK` response can contain valid or invalid XML. Make sure
to design your application to parse the contents of the response and handle it appropriately.
Objects are returned sorted in ascending order of the respective key names in the list. To use this
operation, you must have READ access to the bucket. To use this operation in an AWS Identity and
Access Management (IAM) policy, you must have permissions to perform the s3:ListBucket action. The
bucket owner has this permission by default and can grant this permission to others.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name to list.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Character you use to group keys.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefix</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limits the response to keys that begin with the specified prefix.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the page size of the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>URL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding type used by Amazon S3 to encode object keys in the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#ApiS3Object">Api S3 Object</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### List Multipart Parts

`<s3:list-parts>`

Lists the parts that are uploaded for a specific multipart upload. This operation must include the
upload ID, which you obtain by sending the initiate multipart upload request. Refer to
[CreateMultipartUpload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/API_CreateMultipartUpload.html)
for more information.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket in which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upload ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Upload ID identifying the multipart upload whose parts are listed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the page size of the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Part number marker</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the part after which listing should begin. Only parts with higher part numbers are listed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#ApiObjectPart">Api Object Part</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

### Put Bucket Acl

`<s3:put-bucket-acl>`

Sets the permissions on an existing bucket using access control lists (ACLs). To set the ACL of a
bucket, you must have the WRITE_ACP permission. You can set a bucketâs permissions in one of two
ways:

- Specify the ACL in the request body.
- Specify permissions using request headers.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Grants</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Grant">Grant</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of grants for this bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Canonical AWS ID of the owner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Put Bucket Cors

`<s3:put-bucket-cors>`

Sets the CORS configuration for your bucket. If the configuration exists, Amazon S3 replaces it.

To use this operation, you need permission to perform the s3:PutBucketCORS action. By default, the
bucket owner has this permission and can grant it to others.

You set this configuration on a bucket so that the bucket can service cross-origin requests. For
example, you might want to enable a request whose origin is `http://www.example.com` to access your
Amazon S3 bucket at `my.example.bucket.com` by using the browserâs XMLHttpRequest capability.

To enable cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) on a bucket, you add the CORS subresource to the
bucket. The CORS subresource is an XML document in which you configure rules that identify origins
and the HTTP methods that can be executed on your bucket. The document is limited to 64 KB in size.
When Amazon S3 receives a cross-origin request (or a pre-flight OPTIONS request) against a bucket,
it evaluates the CORS configuration on the bucket and uses the first CORSRule rule that matches the
incoming browser request to enable a cross-origin request. For a rule to match, the following
conditions must be met:

- The requestâs origin header must match the AllowedOrigin elements.
- The request method (for example, GET, PUT, HEAD, and so on) or the Access-Control-Request-Method
  header for a pre-flight OPTIONS request must be one of the AllowedMethod elements. Every header
  specified in the Access-Control-Request-Headers request header of a pre-flight request must match
  an AllowedHeader element.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cors Rules</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#CorsRule">Cors Rule</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of CORS rules.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Put Bucket Lifecycle Configuration

`<s3:put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration>`

Creates a new lifecycle configuration for the bucket or replaces an existing lifecycle
configuration. This operation overwrites an existing lifecycle configuration, so if you want to
retain any configuration details, include them in the new lifecycle configuration.

### Put Bucket Logging

`<s3:put-bucket-logging-configuration>`

Set the logging parameters for a bucket and to specify permissions for who can view and modify the
logging parameters. All logs are saved to buckets in the same AWS Region as the source bucket. To
set the logging status of a bucket, you must be the bucket owner. The bucket owner is automatically
granted FULL_CONTROL to all logs. Use the Grantee request element to grant access to other people.
The Permissions request element specifies the kind of access the grantee has to the logs.

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR

- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Put Bucket Notification Configuration

`<s3:put-bucket-notification-configuration>`

Enables notifications of specified events for a bucket. Using this API, you can replace an existing
notification configuration. The configuration is an XML file that defines the event types that you
want Amazon S3 to publish and the destination where you want Amazon S3 to publish an event
notification when it detects an event of the specified type. This operation replaces the existing
notification configuration with the configuration you include in the request. You can disable
notifications by defining an empty notificationConfiguration. By default, only the bucket owner can
configure notifications on a bucket. However, bucket owners can use a bucket policy to grant
permission to other users by setting this configuration with an s3:PutBucketNotification permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Notification Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#NotificationConfiguration">Notification Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration of the notifications behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN

### Put Bucket Policy

`<s3:put-bucket-policy>`

Applies an Amazon S3 bucket policy to an Amazon S3 bucket. If you are using an identity other than
the root user of the AWS account that owns the bucket, the calling identity must have the
PutBucketPolicy permissions on the specified bucket and belong to the bucket ownerâs account to use
this operation.

### Put Bucket Tagging

`<s3:put-bucket-tagging>`

Sets the tags for a bucket. Use tags to organize your AWS bill to reflect your own cost structure.
To do this, sign up to get your AWS account bill with tag key values included. Then, to see the cost
of combined resources, organize your billing information according to resources with the same tag
key values. For example, you can tag several resources with a specific application name, and then
organize your billing information to see the total cost of that application across several services.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Tags</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket tags.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- S3:CONNECTIVITY

- S3:S3_ERROR

### Put Bucket Versioning

`<s3:put-bucket-versioning>`

Sets the versioning state of an existing bucket.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Versioning Status</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Enabled</p></li><li><p>Suspended</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Status of versioning for this bucket.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Enabled</p><div><p>Enables versioning for the objects in the bucket. All objects added to the bucket receive a unique version ID.</p></div></li><li><p>Suspended</p><div><p>Disables versioning for the objects in the bucket. All objects added to the bucket receive a null version ID. If the versioning state has never been set on a bucket, it has no versioning state, so a GetBucketVersioning request does not return a versioning state value.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- S3:CONNECTIVITY

- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Put Bucket Website

`<s3:put-bucket-website>`

Sets the configuration of the website that is specified in the website subresource. To configure a
bucket as a website, you can add this subresource on the bucket with website configuration
information, such as the file name of the index document and any redirect rules.

This PUT action requires the S3:PutBucketWebsite permission. By default, only the bucket owner can
configure the website attached to a bucket; however, bucket owners can allow other users to set the
website configuration by writing a bucket policy that grants them the S3:PutBucketWebsite
permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Website Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#WebsiteConfiguration">Website Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Website serving configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Put Object

`<s3:put-object>`

Adds an object to a bucket. You must have WRITE permissions on a bucket to add an object to it.
Amazon S3 never adds partial objects; if you receive a success response, Amazon S3 adds the entire
object to the bucket.

Amazon S3 is a distributed system. If it receives multiple write requests for the same object
simultaneously, it overwrites all but the last object written. Amazon S3 does not provide object
locking. If you need object locking, make sure to build it into your application layer or use
versioning instead. To ensure that data is not corrupted traversing the network, use the Content-MD5
parameter. When you use this parameter, Amazon S3 checks the object against the provided MD5 value
and, if they do not match, returns an error. Additionally, you can calculate the MD5 while putting
an object to Amazon S3 and compare the returned ETag to the calculated MD5 value.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name to which the PUT action was initiated</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the PUT action was initiated</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of the object to upload.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object ACL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PRIVATE</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ_WRITE</p></li><li><p>AWS_EXEC_READ</p></li><li><p>AUTHENTICATED_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_FULL_CONTROL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Canned ACL to apply to the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom user metadata. Use only US-ASCII characters for user metadata.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the body in bytes. This field is useful when the size of the body cannot be determined automatically.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard MIME type describing the format of the contents.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Language</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Language the content is in.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies what content encodings are applied to the object and thus what decoding mechanisms to apply to obtain the media-type referenced by the <strong>Content Type</strong> header field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cache Control</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies caching behavior along the request or reply chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base64 encoded 128-bit MD5 digest of the associated object (content - not including headers) according to RFC 1864.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Disposition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies presentational information for the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expires</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Date and time at which the object is no longer cacheable.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Storage Class</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>STANDARD</p></li><li><p>REDUCED_REDUNDANCY</p></li><li><p>STANDARD_IA</p></li><li><p>ONEZONE_IA</p></li><li><p>INTELLIGENT_TIERING</p></li><li><p>GLACIER</p></li><li><p>DEEP_ARCHIVE</p></li><li><p>OUTPOSTS</p></li><li><p>GLACIER_IR</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amazon S3 Storage class in which the object is located.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Payer</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Confirms that the requester knows that they are charged for the request. Bucket owners do not need to specify this field in their requests. Valid value are the requesters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the algorithm to use to when encrypting the object. Valid values are AES256.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aws:kms</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Kms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AWS Key Management System key ID used for Server Side Encryption of the Amazon S3 object. You must specify the <strong>Server Side Encryption</strong> field when you set the <strong>Sse Kms Key Id</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Represents a customer-provided key to use with Amazon S3 server-side encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Kms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AWS Key Management System key ID to use for server-side encryption of the Amazon S3 object. The <strong>Server Side Encryption</strong> field must be specified when this field is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Key MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the base64-encoded 128-bit MD5 digest of the encryption key according to RFC 1321. Amazon S3 uses this header for a message integrity check to ensure that the encryption key is transmitted without error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Server Side Encryption</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server side encryption algorithm used when storing this object in Amazon S3. This field sets the HTTP x-amz-server-side-encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) matches the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) is different from the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#PutObjectResponse">Put Object Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Put Object ACL

`<s3:put-object-acl>`

Uses the ACL subresource to set the access control list (ACL) permissions for a new or an existing
object in an S3 bucket. You must have WRITE_ACP permission to set the ACL of an object. You can set
access permissions using one of the following methods:

- Specify an **Object ACL** field. Amazon S3 supports a set of predefined ACLs, known as canned
  ACLs. Each canned ACL has a predefined set of grantees and permissions.
- Specify a list of grants using the **Grants** field. You specify explicit access permissions and
  grantees (AWS accounts or Amazon S3 groups) who receive the permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name that contains the object to which you want to attach the ACL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the action is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base64 encoded 128-bit MD5 digest of the associated object (content - not including headers) according to RFC 1864.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID used to reference a specific version of the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object ACL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PRIVATE</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_READ_WRITE</p></li><li><p>AWS_EXEC_READ</p></li><li><p>AUTHENTICATED_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_READ</p></li><li><p>BUCKET_OWNER_FULL_CONTROL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Canned ACL to apply to the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Owner">Owner</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Account ID of the expected bucket owner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Grants</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Grant">Grant</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of grants.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY

- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
