### Restore Object

`<s3:restore-object>`

Restores an archived copy of an object back into Amazon S3. Objects that you archive to the S3
Glacier or S3 Glacier Deep Archive storage class, and S3 Intelligent-Tiering Archive or S3
Intelligent-Tiering Deep Archive tiers are not accessible in real time.

For objects in Archive Access or Deep Archive Access tiers, you must first initiate a restore
request, and then wait until the object is moved into the Frequent Access tier.

For objects in S3 Glacier or S3 Glacier Deep Archive storage classes, you must first initiate a
restore request, and then wait until a temporary copy of the object is available.

To access an archived object, you must restore the object for the duration (number of days) that you
specify.

To restore a specific object version, you can provide a version ID. If you donât provide a version
ID, Amazon S3 restores the current version.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name containing the object to restore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the action is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID used to reference a specific version of the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Days</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Lifetime of the active copy in days. Do not use with restores that specify OutputLocation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Glacier Job Tier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Standard</p></li><li><p>Bulk</p></li><li><p>Expedited</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retrieval tier at which the restore is processed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#RestoreObjectResponse">Restore Object Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:GLACIER_EXPEDITED_RETRIEVAL_NOT_AVAILABLE
- S3:INVALID_OBJECT_STATE
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY
- S3:OBJECT_ALREADY_IN_ACTIVE_TIER
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RESTORE_ALREADY_IN_PROGRESS
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Select Object Content

`<s3:select-object-content>`

Filters the contents of an Amazon S3 object based on a simple structured query language (SQL)
statement.

In the request, along with the SQL expression, you must also specify a data serialization format
(JSON, CSV, or Apache Parquet) of the object. Amazon S3 uses this format to parse object data into
records, and returns only records that match the specified SQL expression.

You must also specify the data serialization format for the response. You can use the Amazon S3
Select Object operation to query objects formatted as CSV, JSON, or Parquet files.

This operation supports the following compression types:

- GZIP and BZIP2 for CSV and JSON files
- Columnar compression for Parquet files using GZIP or Snappy

The Amazon S3 **Select Object** operation does not support whole-object compression for Parquet
files.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name containing the object content to select.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key containing the content to select.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that is used to query the object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expression Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of the provided expression.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Serialization</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ApiInputSerialization">Api Input Serialization</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes the format of the data in the object that is being queried.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Serialization</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ApiOutputSerialization">Api Output Serialization</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes the format of the data that you want Amazon S3 to return in response.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scan Range</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ApiScanRange">Api Scan Range</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the byte range of the object to get the records from. A record is processed when its first byte is contained by the range.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Progress Enabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if periodic request progress information must be enabled.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server-side encryption (SSE) algorithm used to encrypt the object. This field is required only when the object is created using a checksum algorithm.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server-side encryption (SSE) customer managed key. This field is required only when the object is created using a checksum algorithm.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Key MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MD5 server-side encryption (SSE) customer managed key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:NO_SUCH_BUCKET
- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Upload Part

`<s3:upload-part>`

Uploads a part in a multipart upload.

In this operation, you provide part data in your request. However, you have an option to specify
your existing Amazon S3 object as a data source for the part you are uploading.

To upload a part from an existing object, use the [Upload Part Copy](#UploadPartCopy) operation. You
must initiate a multipart upload (refer to [Create Multipart Upload](#CreateMultipartUpload)) before
you can upload any part.

In response to your initiate request, Amazon S3 returns an upload ID and an unique identifier that
you must include in your upload part request. Part numbers can be any number from 1 to 10,000,
inclusive. A part number uniquely identifies a part and also defines its position within the object
created. If you upload a new part using the same part number that is used with a previous part, the
previously uploaded part is overwritten. Each part must be at least 5 MB in size, except the last
part. There is no size limit on the last part of your multipart upload.

To ensure that data is not corrupted when traversing the network, specify the Content-MD5 header in
the upload part request. Amazon S3 checks the part data against the provided MD5 value. If they do
not match, Amazon S3 returns an error.

> [!NOTE] After you initiate a multipart upload and upload one or more parts, you must either
> complete or abort the multipart upload to stop getting charged for storing the uploaded parts.
> After you complete or abort the multipart upload, Amazon S3 frees up the parts storage and stops
> charging you for it.

#### Common Cases for the Operation

- When you upload content of a large file in parts (for example, an FTP Connector **Read** operation
  or SFTP Connector **On New or Updated File** source of a Mule app), you must configure the **non
  repeatable stream** strategy in these operations. You must also specify the number of bytes to
  consume from the content in the **Upload part > Content length** field.
- When you upload the content of many small files, you can configure either the **repeatable
  stream** or **non repeatable stream** strategy in the FTP or SFTP Connector operations. In the
  **Upload part** operation, you can optionally specify the entire file size in the **Content
  length** field.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the bucket in which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upload Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Upload ID identifying the multipart upload whose part is uploaded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Part Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Part number of part uploaded. This is a positive integer between 1 and 10,000.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object data.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base64 encoded 128-bit MD5 digest of the associated object (content - not including headers) according to RFC 1864.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the body in bytes. This field is useful when the size of the body cannot be determined automatically.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#UploadPartResponse">Upload Part Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- S3:BAD_REQUEST
- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN
- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- S3:S3_ERROR
- S3:SERVER_BUSY
- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

### Upload Part Copy

`<s3:upload-part-copy>`

Uploads a part by copying data from an existing object as data source. The minimum allowable part
size for a multipart upload is 5 MB. Instead of using an existing object as part data, you can use
the [Upload Part](#UploadPart) operation and provide data in your request. You must initiate a
multipart upload before you can upload a part. Amazon S3 returns a unique identifier, the upload ID,
that you must include in your upload part request.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the source bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the source key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination bucket name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination object key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object key for which the multipart upload is initiated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Upload Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Upload ID identifying the multipart upload whose part is copied.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Part number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Part number of part copied. This is a positive integer between 1 and 10,000.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bytes range</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Range of the bytes that are copied from the source object from the first byte to the last byte.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) matches the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not matching ETag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if its entity tag (ETag) is different from the specified tag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Modified since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if it was modified since the specified time.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Unmodified since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Matches the object if it was not modified since the specified time.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- S3:CONNECTIVITY
- S3:FORBIDDEN

### Api Checksum

Configures the API checksum type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum CRC32</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum CRC32.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum CRC32 C</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum CRC32 C.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum SHA1</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum SHA1.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum SHA256</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum SHA256.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Api Copy Object Result

Configures the API copy object result type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>E Tag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ETag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum CRC32</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum CRC32.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum CRC32 C</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum CRC32 C.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum SHA1</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum SHA1.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Checksum SHA256</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checksum SHA256.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Last Modified</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Last modified.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Api CSV Input

Configures the API CSV input type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>File Header Info</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USE</p></li><li><p>IGNORE</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>File header information.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Comments</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comments.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Quote Escape Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Quote escape character.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Record Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Record delimiter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Field delimiter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Quote Character</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Quote character.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Allow Quoted Record Delimiter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Allow quoted record delimiter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Condition

Configures the condition type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Http Error Code Returned Equals</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP error code returned equals.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Prefix Equals</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key prefix equals.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Grantee

Configures the grantee type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CANONICAL_USER</p></li><li><p>AMAZON_CUSTOMER_BY_EMAIL</p></li><li><p>GROUP</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Email Address</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URI.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

Configures a proxy for outbound connections.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname or IP address of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Non-proxy hosts.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ntlm Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NTLM domain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Put Object Response

Configures the put object response type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>E Tag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ETag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Key Charged</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Request charged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Server Side Encryption</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server-side encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse customer key MD5.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ssekms Encryption ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Queue Configuration

Configures the queue configuration type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Queue ARN.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Events</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Events.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Notification Filter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#NotificationConfigurationFilter">Notification Configuration Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Notification filter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine if a message was redelivered. This property can be set only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

Configures how to identify a redelivered message and how to find out when the message was
redelivered.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message was redelivered. You can set this property only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redirect

Configures the redirect type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Http Redirect Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP redirect code.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Protocol.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Key Prefix With</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Replace key prefix.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replace Key With</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Replace key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redirect Rule

Configures the redirect rule type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Hostname</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Protocol.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

Configures the repeatable file store iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Objects</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

Configures the repeatable in memory iterable type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of instances that is initially allowed to keep in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong> attribute, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>100</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>100</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, <strong>Max Buffer Size</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Restore Object Response

Configures the restore object response type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Request Charged</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Request charged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Restore Output Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Restore output path.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Role

Configures the Role type.

> [!NOTE] The Role connection type is supported only for standalone, CloudHub 2.0, or Runtime Fabric
> deployments. The Role connection type is not supported for CloudHub deployments.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ARN.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>External ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Duration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Duration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>3600</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Duration Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Duration</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Referred Policy Arns</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Referred policy ARNs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tags</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Tags.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Sts Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom STS endpoint. The custom STS endpoint might not work if <code>DefaultAWSCredentialsProviderChain</code> is used. Instead, use AWS environment variables such as <code>AWS_STS_REGIONAL_ENDPOINTS</code>, <code>AWS_REGION</code>, and <code>AWS_ENDPOINT_URL</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Routing Rule

Configures the routing rule type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Condition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Condition">Condition</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Condition.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redirect</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Redirect">Redirect</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Redirect.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### S3 Key Filter

Configures the S3 key filter type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Filter Rules</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#FilterRule">Filter Rule</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Filter rules.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### S3 Object Trigger Response

Configures the S3 object trigger response type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>E Tag</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ETag.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Owner">Owner</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Owner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sequencer</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sequencer.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tag

Configures the tag type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key for this type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value for this type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Topic Configuration

Configures the topic configuration type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic ARN.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Events</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Events.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Notification Filter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#NotificationConfigurationFilter">Notification Configuration Filter</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Notification filter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Transition

Configures the transition type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Date.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Days</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Days.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Storage Class</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>STANDARD</p></li><li><p>REDUCED_REDUNDANCY</p></li><li><p>STANDARD_IA</p></li><li><p>ONEZONE_IA</p></li><li><p>INTELLIGENT_TIERING</p></li><li><p>GLACIER</p></li><li><p>DEEP_ARCHIVE</p></li><li><p>OUTPOSTS</p></li><li><p>GLACIER_IR</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Storage class.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Upload Part Copy Response

Configures the upload part copy response type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket Key Enabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Bucket key enabled.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Copy Part Result</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ApiCopyPartResult">Api Copy Part Result</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Copy the part result.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Copy Source Version Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Copy the source version ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Charged</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Request charged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Server Side Encryption</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Server-side encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SSE customer algorithm.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sse Customer Key MD5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SSE customer key MD5.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ssekms Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SSEKMS key ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Amazon S3 Connector 8.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure S3 storage](#configure-storage).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Amazon S3 Reference](amazon-s3-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Amazon S3 to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs
namespace and schema location and to add the required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `amazon s3` in the search field.
3.  Click **Amazon S3** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Amazon S3:

- **On Deleted Object**

  Initiates access to your app when an Amazon S3 object is deleted

- **On New Object**

  Initiates access to your app when an Amazon S3 object is created

For example, to configure an **On Deleted Object** source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon S3 > On Deleted Object**.
2.  Drag **On Deleted Object** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **On Deleted Object** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display
    Name** field.
4.  Specify values for the following fields:
    - **Bucket name**  
      Name of the bucket containing the multipart upload to abort
    - **Notification queue name**  
      Amazon SQS queue name used to notify users when a new object is created or deleted in an S3
      bucket. If omitted, a default queue name with prefix
      `mule-s3-trigger-<event-type>-queue-<bucket-name>-folder-` is created and used. Where
      even-type is either `new` or `deleted`. Folder name specified in the `General` section of
      configuration on which event is triggered, will be used as the queue name. If any special
      character other than `a-z` , `A-Z` , `0-9` , `_` , `-` is used in folder name, then from
      default queue name these characters will be replaced by `_` . Also if length of the default
      queue name goes beyond `80 in size`, rest will be trimmed off. NOTE: As the default queue is
      created (if not specified) and the source uses SQS:Received to poll for the event
      notifications connector needs following SQS permissions: sqs:CreateQueue, sqs:
      GetQueueAttributes, sqs: SetQueueAttributes, sqs: GetQueueUrl, sqs: ReceiveMessage, sqs:
      DeleteMessage\*
    - **Scheduling Strategy**  
      Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling

5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the **On Deleted Object** source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
8.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
9.  Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for Amazon S3, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon S3** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target Amazon S3 system. Amazon S3 supports basic and role
authentication.

To configure the global element for Amazon S3 Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the
    [Connection (S3) Connection Type](#connection) method to configure.

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, see
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

    Validating a connection with **Test Connection** requires that you have permission in AWS IAM to
    the action `s3:ListAllMyBuckets`. If you donât have this permission, the test fails. However,
    you can still use the connector and the operations to which you have access.

    Access to operations on Amazon S3 is further controlled through policies. It is not always
    possible to validate your credentials before the exact operation for which you have access
    completes. This can vary based on the bucket name and other parameters. For example, the test
    connection can fail if your credentials have a restricted policy.

### Connection (S3) Connection Type

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure the Connection (S3) connection type:

-

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Select <code>Connection</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

-

## Example of Configuring S3-Compatible Storage

The [MinIO Project](https://github.com/minio/) is one example of Amazon S3 storage. If you configure
this storage locally, set the S3 Compatible Storage URL to `http://127.0.0.1:9000`.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## Next Steps

After configuring Studio, see the [Examples](amazon-s3-connector-examples) topic for more
configuration ideas.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Amazon S3 Connector Reference](amazon-s3-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Troubleshooting Amazon S3 Connector 8.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Anypoint Connector for Amazon S3 (Amazon S3 Connector), become familiar with the
information about interpreting commonly thrown exception messages.

## Enable SSL Logging

Enable SSL logs to investigate an issue related to TLS communications.

Set your SSL log level depending on your required level of detail:

1.  Enable only SSL log details:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl`

2.  Enable only handshaking details. (The handshake protocol is a series of messages exchanged over
    the record protocol.)

    `javax.net.debug=ssl:handshake`

3.  Enable handshaking details and SSL details at the same time:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl, handshake`

4.  Enable the dumping of all details and traffic data:

    `javax.net.debug=all`

    > [!NOTE] This option is very verbose and under normal circumstances is not necessary.

> [!WARNING] SSL logging results in a performance impact for HTTPS or other TLS connections. Enable
> SSL logging only to troubleshoot a specific issue and do not enable it for extended periods. SSL
> logging produces a significant amount of log messages and can inundate your log file if it is left
> enabled and unattended.

To enable SSL logs:

Set the debug parameter as an argument in the runtime configuration for the application.

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and add `-M-Djavax.net.debug=ssl` in the **VM arguments** section.

## Enable Verbose Exception Logging

Enable verbose exception logs to show a complete stack trace of the error instead of the default
truncated output:

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and append the arguments in the **VM arguments** section to add the
    `mule.verbose.exceptions` property. For example:

    `-XX:PermSize=128M -XX:MaxPermSize=256M -Dmule.verbose.exceptions=true`

## Enable Verbose Logging

To get a better understanding of why an applicationâs interaction with Amazon S3 Connector is
failing, temporarily enable verbose logging for the connector.

Remember to always disable enhanced verbosity after troubleshooting, because it can affect your Mule
applicationâs performance.

To enable verbose logging in the configuration file:

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add an `<AsyncLogger>` tag inside the `<Loggers>` tag:

    ```xml
    			<Loggers>
    				...
    				<AsyncLogger name="org.mule.extension.s3" level="DEBUG"/>
    				...
    			</Loggers>
    ```

6.  Save your application changes.
7.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

## Understand Commonly Thrown Exceptions

Here is a list of commonly thrown exception messages and how to interpret them:

- S3:BAD_REQUEST

  The input or payload is invalid.

- S3:BUCKET_ALREADY_EXISTS

  The bucket name you specify already exists.

- S3:BUCKET_ALREADY_OWNED_BY_YOU

  The bucket you create already exists and is owned by you.

- S3:CONNECTIVITY

  A connection cannot be established.

- S3:FORBIDDEN

  There is a \`403\` forbidden error, for example, there are missing permissions.

- S3:GLACIER_EXPEDITED_RETRIEVAL_NOT_AVAILABLE

  Expedited retrieval is not available.

- S3:INTERNAL_ERROR

  There is an internal error, such as a server issue or a temporary glitch.

- S3:INVALID_OBJECT_STATE

  The object state is invalid with the action.

- S3:MALFORMED_XML

  The XML you send is malformed.

- S3:NO_SUCH_KEY

  The key you specify does not exist.

- S3:NO_SUCH_LIFECYCLE_CONFIGURATION

  The lifecycle configuration you specify does not exist.

- S3:NO_SUCH_TAG_SET

  The tag set you specify does not exist.

- S3:NO_SUCH_UPLOAD

  The upload you specify does not exist.

- S3:OBJECT_ALREADY_IN_ACTIVE_TIER

  The object is already in the active tier.

- S3:OPERATION_ABORTED

  The operation is aborted because there is another in-progress action against the same resource.

- S3:RESTORE_ALREADY_IN_PROGRESS

  The restore is already in progress.

- S3:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

  The maximum number of retries for the operation is reached.

- S3:S3_ERROR

  An error occurs in Amazon S3.

- S3:SERVER_BUSY

  The server is busy.

- S3:SERVICE_ERROR

  There is a service error.

- S3:REQUEST_TIMEOUT

  The Put Object operation had a request timeout.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Amazon S3 Connector Reference](amazon-s3-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
